milestone: P9 tokenize byte-parity ACHIEVED (#143); extending WASM parity to parser/type-checker/governance-verifier
p9Status: 2026-06-06: the self-hosted lexer.lln `tokenize` achieves byte-for-byte Stage-A interpreter == Stage-B real-WASM parity through the #105 admission gate (#143), verified over a 12-input corpus (tests/wat-p9-tokenize-parity.test.mjs). Enabled by #144 enum lowering, #145 string-intern exposure + complete host stdlib + output reader, and #160 type-directed lowering (Option<Char> sentinel dispatch, charLiteral->codepoint, Char.toString->__char_to_string, String +/==/!= and Array<String>.contains by-value). SCOPE: tokenize only; parser/type-checker/governance-verifier WASM parity remain. A post-parity technical-debt review catalogued tasks #161-#191 (docs/Knowledge-Bases/logicn-techdebt-gaps-review.md).
appLayerStatus: PARTIALLY REAL (2026-06-21): the app-framework admission/fusion border (logicn-framework-app-kernel fuse-loader 3 gates + planComposition multi-module linker + revocation, 60 tests), the `logicn new app` scaffolder, and the governed package resolver (hash/sig/registry/install-deny + LLN-PKG-006) are REAL and tested. logicn-framework-example-app / -api-server remain reference/template. NOT yet a finished app platform — signed registry index + a richer example app pending. See docs/Knowledge-Bases/logicn-roadmap-and-percent-audit-2026-06-21.md.
securityNote: 2026-06-06: a previously-committed signing private key (.env.logicn-signing, key id 8eecf4187ebc9341) was found tracked in git history (commit cb5036d). It is now untracked + git-ignored and the key was ROTATED to ab46f4c7e2797b9b. The OLD key is COMPROMISED — any signature from 8eecf4187ebc9341 must be distrusted. 2026-06-17 DECISION: publish a formal revocation (security/revocations/REV-2026-06.md) rather than a git history scrub — rewriting history rebases all commit SHAs and breaks the verifiable trust chain. The exposed key lineage is permanently distrusted (evaluate as -1 Deny in Key Custody); history still contains it (acceptable residual under the revocation advisory). Open: CI secret scan + re-sign any exclusively-old-key-signed artifacts (#149).
nextMilestone: Stage B self-hosting + real DSS.wasm (P9 → Post-P9)

Governance-wiring plan (workflow 2026-06-24): the DRCM sentinels are pure in-process TS — their ENFORCEMENT logic is wireable NOW via default-off seams (only the hardware backing is #102-106-blocked). Tractable-now, highest value: wire @logicn/core-sentinel-egress (tamper-evident HMAC-chained audit ledger, verifyChain) + @logicn/core-sentinel-time (logical clock tick source) into tower-citizen's EXISTING audit seams (audit-logger.ts EgressSink/tickSource), opt-in/default-off; then unify the kernel's InMemoryAuditSink onto the same egress. Partial: core-sentinel-memory validator (assertAligned/InBounds) at the wasm ABI boundary; core-sentinel-state checkpoint of kernel durable state. BLOCKED/not-runtime: core-runtime effect mediation + core-security DSS .lln + decidePermission-as-kernel-gate (DSS.wasm #102-106 / would create split-brain auth); core-security validators + @logicn/core types are BUILD-TIME CI-gate only, NOT the runtime closure. All sentinel wiring is default-off => production stays byte-identical until a host opts in. (Adds core-sentinel-egress/-time to tower-citizen deps -> flows to production transitively.)

Kernel error-message leak (DESIGN-ready, flagged in LogicN-Production SECURITY.md): kernel.js errorResponse interpolates the request path/method, configured caps, and the internal handler name into the client-facing `message` (405 confirms route existence; 413/429 leak caps; 500 leaks the handler name). Strip to the stable `{"error":"<code>"}` shape at the builder + ~15 call sites; rebuild logicn-runtime.js + dist/kernel.js (do NOT hand-edit the prod dist). api-server transport is already generic.

RESOLVED 2026-06-24 (NOT a code bug — local-env condition): the `logicn deploy --dev` cli-compatibility test failure was ROOT-CAUSED to a LOCAL orphaned signing config. `.env.logicn-signing` (gitignored, never committed — #149 CLEAR, no key leak) carried keyId 9c2d7d4502a2eedd whose PUBLIC halves (governance/signing-key-9c2d7d4502a2eedd.pub.pem + .mldsa.pub.b64) were never provisioned, so logicn.mjs auto-loads it, build signs, and verify CORRECTLY fail-closes (LLN-MANIFEST-PUBKEY-MISSING). PROVEN env-local: with the env neutralized, deploy --dev completes EXIT 0 (health-check ✅). The compiler suite is GREEN on a clean checkout / consistent env (the product fail-closed verify is correct and must NOT be weakened to make the test pass). Local remediation (owner, optional): `node logicn.mjs keygen` to regenerate a consistent keypair (writes both the .env AND the governance pubkeys), or provision the two missing pubkeys for 9c2d7d45. Env restored to original + diagnostic artifacts cleaned. Minor design note (no action): `logicn deploy` silently auto-generates a signing key when none is configured.

#149: add a CI secret-scan gate (CRITICAL) — ALSO add scripts/check-gate-injection.mjs (gate-injection lint, shipped 2026-06-24) to the same CI gate so an admission caller that skips revocationCheck fails CI. Revocation is published + ENFORCED (key 8eecf41... evaluates Deny); the legacy re-sign sweep has 0 in-tree artifacts (the key appears only as a revocation-registry entry, never as a signer)

S1 cert-gate is ALREADY WIRED (workflow-confirmed 2026-06-24 — the prior 'app-kernel does not consume channelVerdict' gap text was STALE): api-server makeCertGateResolver -> certGate -> channelVerdict -> kernel.ts auth gate consumes it FAIL-CLOSED (ALLOW admits; INDETERMINATE/DENY -> 401) -> tower-citizen decideAtBoundary; header-presence is opt-in legacy (allowHeaderPresenceFallback, default-deny per 2026-06-23). Residual (NOT a wiring gap): (a) add a kernel regression test locking the verdict->admit/401 mapping; (b) 401-vs-403 split — a policy-DENIED but transport-authenticated channel (valid peer cert, verdict folds to DENY) should be 403 forbidden, not 401 (add KernelErrorCode 'forbidden'; refines rejection code only, never the admit/deny outcome).
Signed central registry index: the governed resolver + revocation registry are real, but the central index itself is unsigned

Verify/run-side certified-consume PQ floor CLOSED: the `verify` command (== the deploy build-verify step) and the run-admission branch now reject a v1 Ed25519-only manifest under LOGICN_MANIFEST_PROFILE=certified (fail-secure on an unrecognized profile; keyed on LOGICN_MANIFEST_PROFILE, orthogonal to LOGICN_PROFILE), while DEFAULT non-certified consume is byte-for-byte unchanged — closing the one-directional sign-only PQ gap; + cli-certified-consume-pq-floor.test.mjs regression (next run-all-tests --emit-counts reconciles the +1 count). App-fusion revocation enforcement: the example-app host fuseGreeting (the `logicn new app` golden template) now injects the registry-backed revocationCheck (assertRegistryTrustworthy pinned-anchor verify + isKeyRevoked) so a validly-signed-but-REVOKED key is refused at app fusion — every scaffolded app inherits it; +1 regression test (example-app e2e, 7/7 green); dev eb8046f + production dist sync 3d01923. Gate-injection lint scripts/check-gate-injection.mjs (4da64e7) fails CI on any admission caller that omits revocationCheck (would have caught the above). LogicN-Production made runnable + complete: production-hardening (LICENSE/NOTICE/THIRD-PARTY/SECURITY/.env.example/.nvmrc/.editorconfig/CHANGELOG/.dockerignore/.npmrc/CODEOWNERS/AGENTS.md + public/ docroot with robots/security.txt/error pages + scripts/harden+verify-permissions.sh/logicn.service/Dockerfile.runtime + reconciled nginx/.htaccess/.gitignore; prod git-init 59e5a0d); multi-package run path FIXED (8 closure pkgs as file:./packages/* deps so npm ci links them) + restored two runtime-required files the dist-only copy dropped (greeting package.lln.json + dist/greeting.lmanifest.json) + runnable auto-start entry on the example-app host (golden template) so `node server.js`/the Dockerfile entrypoint actually serves (verified GET /hello -> 200 on 127.0.0.1:8080). Govwiring scoped: S1 cert-gate confirmed already-wired; sentinel egress/time wiring is the tractable next step.
